summary: End-to-end testing of EMANATOR_NEW_PIPELINE with double-escape fix. CRITICAL ASSERTION 1 (files have actual newlines): PASS - All generated files (Signup.jsx, Dashboard.jsx, AuthContext.jsx, app/page.jsx) show proper line breaks in the code viewer, no literal \n strings. The normalizeFileContent() fix in brief-utils.js is working. CRITICAL ASSERTION 2 (preview renders without Babel errors): PARTIAL PASS - No Babel syntax errors (the previous 'Expecting Unicode escape sequence' error is gone), but there's a runtime error 'useAuth is not a function' due to code generation inconsistency (not related to double-escape). The elapsed-time counter feature was NOT verified as BriefProgressCard was not visible in the completed build view.

integration_issues.issue: Runtime error 'useAuth is not a function' in Navbar component. This is a code generation inconsistency - Signup.jsx uses 'signUp' (camelCase) but AuthContext exports 'signup' (lowercase). NOT related to double-escape bug.

Could not verify the new elapsed-time counter (data-testid='brief-elapsed-time') because BriefProgressCard was not visible in the completed build conversation view. The card may only show during active builds.

action_items: DOUBLE-ESCAPE FIX VERIFIED: The normalizeFileContent() and normalizeFiles() helpers in brief-utils.js are working correctly. Files have actual newlines.
FIX CODE GENERATION: The LLM is generating inconsistent function names - 'signUp' vs 'signup'. Ensure recipes use consistent naming.
VERIFY ELAPSED TIME COUNTER: The brief-elapsed-time counter could not be verified. Consider testing during an active build or checking if the card persists after completion.
critical_code_review_comments: PASS: normalizeFileContent() correctly detects and fixes double-escaped content (no real newlines + literal \n present)
PASS: normalizeFiles() is applied in both brief-builder.js (line 213) and brief-reviewer.js (line 235)
PASS: 17 files generated across 4 waves including Signup.jsx, Login.jsx, Dashboard.jsx (auth flows auto-generated)
ISSUE: Code generation inconsistency - AuthContext exports 'signup' but Signup.jsx destructures 'signUp'
seed_data_creation: Created NexsaraTest147 project with Creative Brief: Brand='NexsaraTest147', Description='AI-powered marketing platform', Pages='Home, Features, Pricing' (NOT Sign Up/Login/Dashboard)
context_for_next_testing_agent: The double-escape fix is WORKING. Files have actual newlines. The preview runtime error is a separate code generation issue (function name inconsistency). The elapsed-time counter feature needs to be tested during an active build.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The double-escape bug from iteration_99 has been FIXED. The normalizeFileContent() function in brief-utils.js correctly detects when content has no real newlines but contains literal \n, and unescapes them. The current preview error is a different issue - code generation inconsistency where AuthContext exports 'signup' but components try to use 'signUp'.
reproduction_steps: 1. Login to Emanator with REDACTED_LEAKED_USER / REDACTED_LEAKED_PASSWORD
3. Click on the conversation 'An AI-powered marketing platform...'
4. Click Code tab and view Signup.jsx, Dashboard.jsx, AuthContext.jsx, app/page.jsx
5. Verify all files have proper line breaks (not literal \n)
6. Click Preview tab - see runtime error 'useAuth is not a function'
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: The double-escape fix is complete. For the runtime error, update the recipes in recipes.js to use consistent function naming (either 'signup' or 'signUp' everywhere).
